name: Phenol
acute_effects: Severe burns to skin and eyes; systemic toxicity including CNS depression, cardiac arrhythmia, and organ damage upon skin absorption; can be fatal
chronic_effects: Liver and kidney damage; dermatitis with repeated exposure
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood. Phenol is readily absorbed through skin; double gloving recommended.
storage_requirements: Store in tightly closed container, protected from light, at room temperature. Store in corrosives cabinet.
spill_procedure: Evacuate area. Wear full PPE. Absorb with vermiculite or dry sand. Collect in hazardous waste container. Decontaminate area with PEG 300/400.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste. Do not pour down drain. Label as toxic/corrosive organic waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. If breathing is difficult, give oxygen. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing immediately. Flush with copious amounts of water for at least 15 minutes. Apply PEG 300 or PEG 400 if available. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes, lifting upper and lower eyelids. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Rinse mouth with water. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 5 ppm TWA (skin). ACGIH TLV: 5 ppm TWA (skin).

name: Chloroform
acute_effects: CNS depression, dizziness, nausea, headache; liver and kidney damage at high exposure; respiratory irritation
chronic_effects: Liver and kidney damage; reasonably anticipated to be a human carcinogen (IARC Group 2B)
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood. Chloroform vapors are heavier than air and can accumulate at floor level.
storage_requirements: Store in tightly sealed amber glass bottles in a cool, well-ventilated area away from light. Store separately from oxidizers and strong bases.
spill_procedure: Evacuate area. Ventilate. Absorb with inert material. Collect in sealed container for hazardous waste disposal.
waste_disposal: Collect as halogenated organic waste. Do not pour down drain. Do not evaporate in fume hood.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. If breathing has stopped, give artificial respiration. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Wash skin with soap and water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 50 ppm ceiling. ACGIH TLV: 10 ppm TWA. IARC Group 2B possible carcinogen.

name: Methanol
acute_effects: CNS depression, headache, nausea, visual disturbances including blindness; metabolic acidosis; potentially fatal
chronic_effects: Optic nerve damage; vision impairment; CNS effects
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood. Keep away from ignition sources. Ground and bond containers during transfer.
storage_requirements: Store in flammable liquids cabinet away from heat and ignition sources. Keep container tightly closed.
spill_procedure: Remove ignition sources. Ventilate area. Absorb with inert material. Collect for hazardous waste disposal.
waste_disposal: Collect as flammable organic waste. May be incinerated. Do not pour down drain.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water for at least 15 minutes. Remove contaminated clothing.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Seek immediate medical attention. Ethanol may be administered as antidote by medical personnel.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 200 ppm TWA (skin). ACGIH TLV: 200 ppm TWA (skin).

name: Ethanol
acute_effects: CNS depression at high concentrations; eye and respiratory tract irritation
chronic_effects: Chronic overexposure may cause liver damage
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. Use fume hood when heating or using large quantities. Keep away from ignition sources.
storage_requirements: Store in flammable liquids cabinet. Keep away from heat and ignition sources.
spill_procedure: Remove ignition sources. Ventilate. Absorb with inert material or flush small spills with water.
waste_disposal: Collect as flammable organic waste. Small quantities may be flushed with water per institutional guidelines.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Seek medical attention if large quantity consumed.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 1000 ppm TWA. ACGIH TLV: 1000 ppm STEL.

name: Isopropanol
acute_effects: CNS depression; eye and respiratory irritation; nausea, vomiting, headache
chronic_effects: Repeated overexposure may cause dermatitis and CNS effects
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. Use fume hood when heating. Keep away from ignition sources.
storage_requirements: Store in flammable liquids cabinet. Keep container tightly closed.
spill_procedure: Remove ignition sources. Ventilate. Absorb with inert material.
waste_disposal: Collect as flammable organic waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Do not induce vomiting. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 400 ppm TWA. ACGIH TLV: 200 ppm TWA, 400 ppm STEL.

name: Acetone
acute_effects: Eye, nose, and throat irritation; headache, dizziness, CNS depression at high concentrations
chronic_effects: Dermatitis from defatting of skin with repeated exposure
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area or fume hood. Keep away from ignition sources. Ground containers during transfer.
storage_requirements: Store in flammable liquids cabinet. Keep away from heat and ignition sources.
spill_procedure: Remove ignition sources. Ventilate. Absorb with inert material. Collect for hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as flammable organic waste. Do not pour down drain.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Do not induce vomiting. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 1000 ppm TWA. ACGIH TLV: 250 ppm TWA, 500 ppm STEL.

name: Dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO)
acute_effects: Mild skin and eye irritation; rapidly penetrates skin and can carry dissolved substances into the body
chronic_effects: May cause lens opacities with chronic high-dose exposure in animal studies
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. CRITICAL: DMSO rapidly penetrates skin and carries dissolved chemicals through skin barrier. Never handle toxic solutes dissolved in DMSO without appropriate gloves.
storage_requirements: Store at room temperature in tightly closed container. Freezing point is 18.5°C; store above this temperature for liquid form.
spill_procedure: Absorb with inert material. Wash area with water. Collect for waste disposal.
waste_disposal: Collect as organic waste. Small quantities may be disposed per institutional guidelines.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash immediately and thoroughly with soap and water. Ensure no toxic substances were dissolved in DMSO.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: No OSHA PEL established. Not classified as carcinogenic.

name: Formamide
acute_effects: Skin and eye irritation; may cause CNS effects at high exposure
chronic_effects: Reproductive toxicity; teratogenic in animal studies. Suspected of damaging fertility and the unborn child.
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood, especially when heating. Avoid skin contact.
storage_requirements: Store in tightly closed container at room temperature. Protect from light.
spill_procedure: Ventilate area. Absorb with inert material. Collect for hazardous waste disposal.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous organic waste. Do not pour down drain.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Wash skin thoroughly with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: EU CLP: Repr. 1B (H360D). Reproductive toxicant.

name: Xylene
acute_effects: CNS depression; headache, dizziness, nausea; respiratory and eye irritation
chronic_effects: Liver and kidney damage; CNS effects; dermatitis
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood. Keep away from ignition sources.
storage_requirements: Store in flammable liquids cabinet. Keep container tightly closed.
spill_procedure: Remove ignition sources. Ventilate. Absorb with inert material. Collect for hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as flammable organic waste. Do not pour down drain.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Wash skin with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting (aspiration hazard). Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 100 ppm TWA. ACGIH TLV: 100 ppm TWA, 150 ppm STEL.

name: Toluene
acute_effects: CNS depression; headache, dizziness, nausea; eye and respiratory irritation
chronic_effects: Reproductive toxicity; liver and kidney damage; CNS damage with chronic exposure
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood. Keep away from ignition sources.
storage_requirements: Store in flammable liquids cabinet. Keep container tightly closed.
spill_procedure: Remove ignition sources. Ventilate. Absorb with inert material. Collect for hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as flammable organic waste. Do not pour down drain.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Wash skin with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting (aspiration hazard). Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 200 ppm TWA. ACGIH TLV: 20 ppm TWA. Reproductive toxicant.

name: Ethidium bromide
acute_effects: Irritation of skin, eyes, and respiratory tract; toxic if swallowed
chronic_effects: Potent mutagen; DNA intercalator. May cause heritable genetic damage. Suspected reproductive toxicant.
engineering_controls: Use in designated area. Double-glove when handling concentrated solutions. UV protective eyewear when visualizing gels.
storage_requirements: Store in tightly closed container at room temperature, protected from light. Keep in designated mutagen storage area.
spill_procedure: Wear double gloves. Absorb with wet paper towels. Decontaminate area. EtBr destaining bags may be used for dilute solutions. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste. Charcoal filtration or commercial destaining bags may be used for dilute solutions per institutional guidelines. Concentrated solutions must be collected as hazardous waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash immediately with soap and water for at least 15 minutes. Remove contaminated clothing.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Known mutagen. Handle as potentially carcinogenic. Some institutions are switching to safer alternatives such as SYBR Safe.

name: Acrylamide
acute_effects: Neurotoxicity; skin irritation and sensitization; eye irritation
chronic_effects: Probable human carcinogen (IARC Group 2A); peripheral neuropathy; reproductive toxicity; neurotoxicity cumulative
engineering_controls: Weigh powder in chemical fume hood or glove box. Use pre-made solutions when possible to minimize dust exposure. Unpolymerized acrylamide is the primary hazard.
storage_requirements: Store in tightly closed container at room temperature in a designated area for carcinogens. Protect from light.
spill_procedure: Wear full PPE. Wet powder spills to prevent dust. Absorb with inert material. Collect as hazardous waste. Decontaminate area.
waste_disposal: Unpolymerized: collect as hazardous waste. Polymerized gels: may be disposed as solid waste per institutional guidelines, but check local regulations.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing immediately. Wash skin with soap and water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 0.3 mg/m³ TWA (skin). IARC Group 2A probable carcinogen. Known neurotoxin.

name: Formaldehyde
acute_effects: Severe eye, skin, and respiratory tract irritation; pulmonary edema at high concentrations; allergic sensitization
chronic_effects: Known human carcinogen (IARC Group 1, nasopharyngeal cancer); respiratory sensitization; contact dermatitis
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood at all times. Monitor airborne concentrations. Use formaldehyde-specific exposure monitoring if routine use.
storage_requirements: Store in tightly closed container at room temperature. Do not store below 15°C (may polymerize). Store in ventilated area.
spill_procedure: Evacuate area. Ventilate. Wear full PPE. Absorb with inert material. Decontaminate with sodium bisulfite solution. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste. Do not pour down drain. Label as carcinogenic waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention. Monitor for delayed pulmonary edema.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Wash skin with soap and water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Rinse mouth. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 0.75 ppm TWA, 2 ppm STEL. IARC Group 1 carcinogen. OSHA Formaldehyde Standard 29 CFR 1910.1048.

name: Paraformaldehyde
acute_effects: Severe eye, skin, and respiratory tract irritation; releases formaldehyde when dissolved; allergic sensitization
chronic_effects: Releases formaldehyde; known human carcinogen (IARC Group 1); respiratory sensitization
engineering_controls: Weigh powder and prepare solutions in chemical fume hood. Releases formaldehyde gas when heated or dissolved. Same precautions as formaldehyde.
storage_requirements: Store tightly sealed at room temperature in a dry, well-ventilated area. Store away from oxidizers.
spill_procedure: Wear full PPE. Carefully sweep up solid and place in sealed container. Wet to prevent dust. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste. Label as carcinogenic. Do not pour down drain.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Wash skin with soap and water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Rinse mouth. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Depolymerizes to formaldehyde. Subject to OSHA Formaldehyde Standard 29 CFR 1910.1048. IARC Group 1 carcinogen.

name: Glutaraldehyde
acute_effects: Severe skin and eye burns; respiratory irritation; strong sensitizer
chronic_effects: Respiratory sensitization (occupational asthma); skin sensitization (allergic contact dermatitis)
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood. Strong respiratory and skin sensitizer.
storage_requirements: Store at 2-8°C in tightly closed container. Protect from light.
spill_procedure: Ventilate area. Wear full PPE. Absorb with inert material. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste. Neutralize with glycine solution before disposal per institutional protocol.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Flush with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Do not induce vomiting. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 0.2 ppm ceiling. ACGIH TLV: 0.05 ppm ceiling. Strong respiratory sensitizer.

name: Sodium azide
acute_effects: Highly toxic; hypotension, headache, dizziness, nausea; can be rapidly fatal; inhibits cytochrome c oxidase similar to cyanide
chronic_effects: Mutagenic in bacterial assays; chronic low-level exposure may cause headache and hypotension
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood. NEVER acidify solutions containing sodium azide (generates toxic hydrazoic acid gas). Do not use with copper, lead, or brass plumbing (forms explosive metal azides).
storage_requirements: Store in tightly closed container at room temperature. Keep away from acids and metals. Store separately from acids.
spill_procedure: Wear full PPE. Do not acidify. Carefully sweep up solid. Absorb liquid with inert material. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste. Do NOT pour down drain (reacts with copper plumbing to form explosive metal azides). Neutralize with ceric ammonium nitrate or nitrous acid per institutional protocol.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek immediate medical attention. Treat as cyanide-type poisoning.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Wash skin with soap and water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Seek immediate medical attention. Do NOT induce vomiting.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 0.3 mg/m³ ceiling (as HN3). ACGIH TLV: 0.29 mg/m³ ceiling. Highly toxic; LD50 (oral, rat) = 27 mg/kg.

name: Diethyl pyrocarbonate (DEPC)
acute_effects: Severe eye and skin irritation; lachrymator; respiratory irritation
chronic_effects: Suspected carcinogen; reacts with amines in biological systems
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood. Decomposes to ethanol and CO2 upon autoclaving, rendering treated water safe. Lachrymator—avoid vapor exposure.
storage_requirements: Store at 2-8°C in tightly sealed container. Moisture-sensitive; keep dry.
spill_procedure: Ventilate area. Wear full PPE. Absorb with inert material. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste. Autoclaved DEPC-treated water is safe and does not require special disposal.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Wash skin with soap and water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Handle as suspected carcinogen. Always autoclave DEPC-treated solutions before use to decompose residual DEPC.

name: N,N'-Methylenebisacrylamide (Bis-acrylamide)
acute_effects: Skin, eye, and respiratory tract irritation
chronic_effects: May cause sensitization; handle as potential neurotoxin (similar to acrylamide but less potent)
engineering_controls: Weigh powder in chemical fume hood. Use pre-made solutions when possible.
storage_requirements: Store in tightly closed container at room temperature. Protect from light and moisture.
spill_procedure: Wear PPE. Wet powder to prevent dust. Collect with inert absorbent. Dispose as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Unpolymerized: collect as hazardous waste. Polymerized gels: follow institutional guidelines.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Handle with same precautions as acrylamide. Use pre-made solutions when available.

name: Hydrochloric acid
acute_effects: Severe burns to skin and eyes; respiratory tract irritation and damage; pulmonary edema
chronic_effects: Dental erosion; chronic bronchitis; dermatitis
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood, especially concentrated solutions. Always add acid to water, never water to acid.
storage_requirements: Store in corrosives cabinet in acid-resistant secondary containment. Separate from bases and oxidizers.
spill_procedure: Evacuate area. Ventilate. Neutralize carefully with sodium bicarbonate. Absorb with inert material. Collect for waste disposal.
waste_disposal: Neutralize to pH 6-8 before disposal per institutional guidelines, or collect as acid waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Flush with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Rinse mouth. Give water to drink if conscious. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 5 ppm ceiling. ACGIH TLV: 2 ppm ceiling.

name: Sulfuric acid
acute_effects: Severe burns to all tissues; extremely exothermic upon dilution; pulmonary edema
chronic_effects: IARC Group 1 carcinogen (strong inorganic acid mists containing sulfuric acid); chronic respiratory effects; dental erosion
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood. ALWAYS add acid slowly to water with stirring, never add water to concentrated acid (highly exothermic).
storage_requirements: Store in corrosives cabinet in acid-resistant secondary containment. Separate from bases, organic materials, and oxidizers.
spill_procedure: Evacuate area. Wear full PPE including face shield. Neutralize carefully and slowly with sodium bicarbonate. Absorb with dry sand. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Neutralize carefully to pH 6-8 before disposal, or collect as acid waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing immediately. Flush with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Do NOT give anything to drink. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 1 mg/m³ TWA. IARC Group 1 carcinogen (occupational exposure to strong inorganic acid mists).

name: Sodium hydroxide
acute_effects: Severe burns to skin, eyes, and mucous membranes; dissolution of concentrated NaOH is highly exothermic
chronic_effects: Chronic skin exposure may cause dermatitis
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. Use fume hood for concentrated solutions or when generating aerosols. Add pellets slowly to water with stirring (exothermic).
storage_requirements: Store in corrosives cabinet, separate from acids. Keep in polyethylene containers (corrodes glass over time). Keep dry.
spill_procedure: Wear full PPE. Neutralize with dilute acid (e.g., dilute HCl or acetic acid). Absorb with inert material.
waste_disposal: Neutralize to pH 6-8 before disposal, or collect as base waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Flush with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention. Alkali burns to eyes are medical emergencies.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Give water to drink if conscious. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 2 mg/m³ ceiling. ACGIH TLV: 2 mg/m³ ceiling.

name: Potassium hydroxide
acute_effects: Severe burns to skin, eyes, and mucous membranes; dissolution is highly exothermic
chronic_effects: Chronic skin exposure may cause dermatitis
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. Add pellets slowly to water (exothermic).
storage_requirements: Store in corrosives cabinet, separate from acids. Keep dry. Use polyethylene containers.
spill_procedure: Wear full PPE. Neutralize with dilute acid. Absorb with inert material.
waste_disposal: Neutralize to pH 6-8 before disposal, or collect as base waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Flush with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Give water if conscious. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 2 mg/m³ ceiling. ACGIH TLV: 2 mg/m³ ceiling.

name: Acetic acid (glacial)
acute_effects: Severe burns to skin and eyes (glacial/concentrated); respiratory irritation; pulmonary edema at high concentrations
chronic_effects: Dental erosion; chronic bronchitis; skin sensitization
engineering_controls: Use glacial acetic acid in chemical fume hood. Dilute solutions may be used on bench with adequate ventilation.
storage_requirements: Store in corrosives cabinet. Glacial acetic acid freezes at 16.6°C. Keep away from ignition sources.
spill_procedure: Ventilate. Neutralize with sodium bicarbonate. Absorb with inert material.
waste_disposal: Dilute solutions may be neutralized and disposed per institutional guidelines. Concentrated acid: collect as hazardous waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Flush with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention for concentrated acid exposure.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 10 ppm TWA. ACGIH TLV: 10 ppm TWA, 15 ppm STEL.

name: Trifluoroacetic acid (TFA)
acute_effects: Severe burns to skin and eyes; severe respiratory tract irritation; pulmonary edema
chronic_effects: Chronic respiratory effects from repeated exposure
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood at all times. Highly volatile and corrosive.
storage_requirements: Store in corrosives cabinet in tightly sealed fluoropolymer or glass container. Keep cool.
spill_procedure: Evacuate area. Ventilate. Wear full PPE. Neutralize carefully with sodium bicarbonate. Absorb with inert material.
waste_disposal: Collect as halogenated acid waste. Do not pour down drain.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing immediately. Flush with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Rinse mouth. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: No specific OSHA PEL. Treat as highly corrosive. More corrosive than acetic acid.

name: Perchloric acid
acute_effects: Severe burns; explosive when concentrated and heated; strong oxidizer
chronic_effects: Chronic respiratory irritation
engineering_controls: Use ONLY in a dedicated perchloric acid fume hood with wash-down capability. Do NOT use in standard fume hoods (explosive perchlorate salts can accumulate in ductwork).
storage_requirements: Store in glass or compatible container in acid cabinet. Separate from organic materials, dehydrating agents, and reducing agents. Keep below 72% concentration.
spill_procedure: Evacuate area. Wear full PPE. Dilute with large amounts of water. Do NOT use organic absorbents. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as oxidizing acid waste. Keep separate from organic waste. Dilute before disposal.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing immediately. Flush with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 20 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Requires dedicated perchloric acid fume hood. Concentrated (>72%) perchloric acid is a powerful oxidizer and potential explosive.

name: TEMED (N,N,N',N'-Tetramethylethylenediamine)
acute_effects: Corrosive to skin and eyes; respiratory tract irritation; harmful if inhaled
chronic_effects: Repeated exposure may cause skin sensitization
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood or well-ventilated area. Strong, unpleasant odor at low concentrations.
storage_requirements: Store in flammable liquids cabinet. Keep tightly closed under nitrogen. Moisture-sensitive.
spill_procedure: Ventilate area. Absorb with inert material. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous organic waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Wash with soap and water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: No specific OSHA PEL. Used as a catalyst for acrylamide polymerization in gel electrophoresis.

name: Beta-mercaptoethanol (BME, 2-Mercaptoethanol)
acute_effects: Toxic by all routes of exposure; severe irritation of eyes, skin, and respiratory tract; nausea, vomiting, headache; pungent, extremely unpleasant odor
chronic_effects: Liver and kidney damage with repeated exposure
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood at all times. Extremely strong, penetrating odor detectable at very low concentrations.
storage_requirements: Store at 2-8°C in tightly closed container in a well-ventilated area or fume hood. Protect from light and air.
spill_procedure: Evacuate area due to odor. Ventilate. Wear full PPE. Absorb with inert material. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous organic waste. Do not pour down drain.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Wash skin thoroughly with soap and water. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: ACGIH TLV: 0.1 ppm TWA (proposed). Environmentally toxic to aquatic organisms.

name: Dithiothreitol (DTT)
acute_effects: Irritation of skin, eyes, and respiratory tract; harmful if swallowed
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. Fume hood recommended when weighing powder.
storage_requirements: Store desiccated at -20°C. Solutions are unstable and should be prepared fresh. Sensitive to air oxidation.
spill_procedure: Wear PPE. Absorb with inert material. Collect for waste disposal.
waste_disposal: Collect as chemical waste per institutional guidelines.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Less hazardous alternative to beta-mercaptoethanol for many applications.

name: TCEP (Tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine)
acute_effects: Skin and eye irritation; respiratory irritation from powder
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. Weigh powder in fume hood.
storage_requirements: Store desiccated at -20°C or 4°C. Solutions are more stable than DTT but should be stored at -20°C for long-term use.
spill_procedure: Wear PPE. Sweep up solid. Absorb liquid with inert material. Collect for waste disposal.
waste_disposal: Collect as chemical waste per institutional guidelines.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.

regulatory_notes: Considered a safer alternative to BME and DTT. Odorless and more stable.

name: Sodium borohydride
acute_effects: Corrosive to skin and eyes; generates flammable hydrogen gas upon contact with water and acids; toxic if ingested
chronic_effects: Repeated exposure may cause respiratory sensitization
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood. Generates hydrogen gas in contact with water and protic solvents. Keep away from ignition sources.
storage_requirements: Store in cool, dry place. Keep away from water and acids. Store under inert atmosphere if possible.
spill_procedure: Do NOT use water. Sweep up carefully. Cover with dry sand or sodium bicarbonate. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Carefully quench small amounts in cold isopropanol (not water). Collect as hazardous waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing. Flush with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: No specific OSHA PEL. Water-reactive solid. DOT hazard class: 4.3 (dangerous when wet).

name: TRIzol / TRI Reagent
acute_effects: Severe burns (contains phenol and guanidinium thiocyanate); toxic by inhalation and skin absorption; CNS depression
chronic_effects: Contains phenol (liver/kidney damage) and may cause chronic organ damage with repeated exposure
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood at all times. Contains phenol and guanidinium thiocyanate. Double-gloving recommended.
storage_requirements: Store at 2-8°C in tightly closed container. Protect from light.
spill_procedure: Evacuate area. Wear full PPE. Absorb with inert material. Decontaminate with PEG 300 for phenol component. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste. Do NOT pour down drain. Do NOT mix with bleach (releases toxic gas from thiocyanate component).

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing immediately. Flush with water for at least 15 minutes. Apply PEG 300 if available. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Rinse mouth. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Combination reagent containing phenol and guanidinium thiocyanate. Follow safety guidelines for both components.

name: Guanidinium thiocyanate (guanidine thiocyanate)
acute_effects: Toxic if swallowed or inhaled; irritating to skin, eyes, and respiratory tract; releases toxic gases when mixed with bleach or acids
chronic_effects: Repeated exposure may cause organ damage
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood when weighing powder. NEVER mix with bleach (generates toxic HCN and COS gases) or acids.
storage_requirements: Store at room temperature in tightly closed container. Hygroscopic; keep dry.
spill_procedure: Wear PPE. Sweep up solid carefully. Absorb liquid with inert material. Do NOT use bleach to clean. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste. NEVER pour down drain or mix with bleach waste. Thiocyanate reacts with bleach to produce toxic gases.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Commonly used in RNA extraction. CRITICAL: never mix with bleach or acids due to toxic gas generation.

name: Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (PCI, 25:24:1)
acute_effects: Severe burns from phenol; toxic vapors from chloroform; CNS depression; phenol rapidly absorbed through skin causing systemic toxicity
chronic_effects: Chloroform is IARC Group 2B possible carcinogen; phenol causes liver/kidney damage; chronic organ damage
engineering_controls: Use in chemical fume hood at all times. Double-gloving strongly recommended. This is one of the most hazardous common lab reagents.
storage_requirements: Store at 2-8°C in tightly closed amber glass bottle. Protect from light.
spill_procedure: Evacuate area. Wear full PPE including face shield. Absorb with vermiculite. Apply PEG 300 to phenol-contaminated surfaces. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as halogenated organic hazardous waste. Do not pour down drain. Do not evaporate in fume hood.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Remove contaminated clothing immediately. Flush with copious water for at least 15 minutes. Apply PEG 300/400 if available. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Contains both phenol (OSHA PEL 5 ppm, skin) and chloroform (OSHA PEL 50 ppm ceiling). Must be used in fume hood.

name: Sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS)
acute_effects: Skin and severe eye irritation; respiratory irritation from powder; harmful if swallowed
chronic_effects: Dermatitis with repeated skin exposure
engineering_controls: Weigh powder in fume hood or use dust mask to prevent inhalation of fine powder. SDS powder is a significant inhalation hazard.
storage_requirements: Store at room temperature in tightly closed container. Keep dry. Powder is flammable.
spill_procedure: Avoid generating dust. Sweep up carefully. Absorb liquid with inert material.
waste_disposal: Dilute solutions may be disposed via drain with water per institutional guidelines. Collect concentrated solutions as waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Drink water. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Fine powder is a dust explosion hazard. Use appropriate precautions when weighing.

name: Triton X-100
acute_effects: Eye and skin irritation; harmful if swallowed
chronic_effects: Contains ethoxylated octylphenol; environmental endocrine disruptor concerns
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. Standard bench work acceptable for dilute solutions.
storage_requirements: Store at room temperature in tightly closed container.
spill_procedure: Absorb with inert material. Wash area with water. Collect for waste disposal. Very slippery when wet.
waste_disposal: Collect as chemical waste. Some institutions restrict drain disposal due to environmental concerns (endocrine disruptor).

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: SVHC (Substance of Very High Concern) under EU REACH due to endocrine-disrupting properties. Some labs are switching to Tween-20 or other alternatives.

name: DAPI (4',6-Diamidino-2-phenylindole)
acute_effects: Irritation of skin and eyes; harmful if swallowed or inhaled
chronic_effects: DNA-binding fluorescent dye; potential mutagen. Handle as mutagenic substance.
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. Weigh powder in fume hood. Protect from light during use.
storage_requirements: Store desiccated at -20°C, protected from light. Stock solutions stored at 2-8°C protected from light.
spill_procedure: Wear PPE. Absorb with wet paper towels. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste (potential mutagen).

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: DNA-binding compound. Handle as potential mutagen. Used at very low concentrations (typically 0.1-1 µg/mL).

name: Propidium iodide
acute_effects: Irritation of skin and eyes; harmful if swallowed
chronic_effects: DNA intercalator; potential mutagen and reproductive toxicant. Handle as mutagenic substance.
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. Protect from light during use.
storage_requirements: Store at 2-8°C, protected from light in tightly closed container.
spill_procedure: Wear PPE. Absorb with wet paper towels. Collect as hazardous waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste (potential mutagen).

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: DNA intercalator. Handle as potential mutagen. Commonly used in flow cytometry and cell viability assays.

name: SYBR Green I
acute_effects: Mild skin and eye irritation
chronic_effects: Nucleic acid binding dye; potential mutagen although less mutagenic than ethidium bromide in Ames test
engineering_controls: Standard bench work. Protect from light during use.
storage_requirements: Store at -20°C, protected from light.
spill_procedure: Wear gloves. Absorb with paper towels. Collect for waste disposal.
waste_disposal: Collect as chemical waste per institutional guidelines. Some institutions allow drain disposal of very dilute solutions.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Considered a safer alternative to ethidium bromide for nucleic acid staining, though still a DNA-binding dye.

name: Crystal violet (Gentian violet)
acute_effects: Skin and eye irritation; stains skin and surfaces; harmful if swallowed
chronic_effects: IARC Group 2B possible carcinogen; animal carcinogen
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated area. Stains aggressively; protect surfaces.
storage_requirements: Store at room temperature in tightly closed container.
spill_procedure: Wear PPE. Absorb with paper towels. Clean with ethanol or bleach solution. Persistent staining expected.
waste_disposal: Collect as hazardous waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water. Staining will persist for several days.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: IARC Group 2B possible carcinogen. Avoid prolonged or repeated exposure.

name: Coomassie Brilliant Blue (G-250/R-250)
acute_effects: Skin and eye irritation; respiratory irritation from powder; stains skin and surfaces
engineering_controls: Weigh powder in fume hood. Note: Coomassie staining solutions often contain methanol and acetic acid — follow safety guidelines for those components.
storage_requirements: Store at room temperature in tightly closed container.
spill_procedure: Wear gloves. Absorb with paper towels or inert material. Persistent blue staining expected.
waste_disposal: Staining solutions containing methanol and acetic acid: collect as hazardous organic waste. Aqueous destain: per institutional guidelines.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.

regulatory_notes: The dye itself has low toxicity. Main hazards come from solvents (methanol, acetic acid) in staining/destaining solutions.

name: Liquid nitrogen
acute_effects: Severe cryogenic burns/frostbite on contact; asphyxiation in enclosed spaces due to oxygen displacement; cold gas can cause respiratory damage
engineering_controls: Use only in well-ventilated areas. Install oxygen monitors in rooms where large quantities are stored or used. Never seal liquid nitrogen in closed containers (explosion risk from gas expansion).
storage_requirements: Store in approved cryogenic Dewar vessels only. Never use sealed containers. Ensure adequate ventilation. 1 liter of liquid nitrogen produces approximately 700 liters of gas.
incompatible_with: organic materials at cryogenic temperatures (may become brittle)
spill_procedure: Evacuate enclosed areas immediately (asphyxiation risk). Ventilate area. Allow to evaporate. Do not touch with bare skin. Monitor oxygen levels before re-entry.
waste_disposal: Allow to evaporate in well-ventilated area. No hazardous waste disposal required.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air immediately. If not breathing, give artificial respiration. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Do NOT rub affected area. Immerse in warm (not hot) water (40-42°C). Do not apply direct heat. Seek medical attention for frostbite.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush with lukewarm water. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Not applicable.

regulatory_notes: Simple asphyxiant. OSHA requires oxygen monitoring in confined spaces. Expansion ratio ~1:694.

name: Dry ice (Solid carbon dioxide)
acute_effects: Cryogenic burns/frostbite on contact (-78.5°C); CO2 asphyxiation in enclosed spaces; dizziness, headache from elevated CO2
engineering_controls: Use in well-ventilated areas. CO2 is heavier than air and accumulates at floor level. Never store in sealed containers (sublimation pressure can cause explosion). Never transport in closed vehicles.
storage_requirements: Store in insulated, ventilated containers. Never store in sealed/airtight containers. Store in well-ventilated areas.
spill_procedure: Ventilate area. Allow to sublimate. Do not touch with bare skin. Monitor for CO2 buildup in enclosed areas.
waste_disposal: Allow to sublimate in well-ventilated area. No hazardous waste disposal required.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. If not breathing, give artificial respiration. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Do NOT rub affected area. Immerse in warm (not hot) water. Seek medical attention for frostbite.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush with lukewarm water. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Seek immediate medical attention. Internal frostbite risk.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL for CO2: 5000 ppm TWA. CO2 is an asphyxiant at high concentrations. Sublimation temperature: -78.5°C.

name: Hydrogen peroxide (30%)
acute_effects: Burns to skin and eyes at high concentrations; bleaching of skin; respiratory irritation from vapors
chronic_effects: Repeated exposure may cause skin irritation and bleaching of hair
engineering_controls: Use concentrated solutions (>8%) in fume hood. Strong oxidizer — keep away from combustible materials.
storage_requirements: Store in original vented container at 2-8°C or room temperature depending on concentration. Keep away from combustible materials, metals, and reducing agents.
spill_procedure: Wear PPE. Dilute with large amounts of water. Keep away from combustible materials. Collect for waste disposal.
waste_disposal: Dilute solutions (<3%) may be flushed with water per institutional guidelines. Concentrated: collect as oxidizer waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention.
first_aid.skin_contact: Flush immediately with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention for concentrated solution burns.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek immediate medical attention.
first_aid.ingestion: Do NOT induce vomiting. Rinse mouth. Seek immediate medical attention.

regulatory_notes: OSHA PEL: 1 ppm TWA. ACGIH TLV: 1 ppm TWA. Concentrations >8% are corrosive.

name: Ammonium persulfate (APS)
acute_effects: Skin and eye irritation; respiratory sensitization; may trigger asthma attacks in sensitized individuals
chronic_effects: Respiratory sensitization (occupational asthma); skin sensitization
engineering_controls: Weigh powder in fume hood. Strong oxidizer; keep away from combustible materials. Respiratory sensitizer — minimize dust exposure.
storage_requirements: Store in cool, dry place in tightly closed container. Keep away from combustible materials and reducing agents.
spill_procedure: Wear PPE. Sweep up carefully, avoiding dust generation. Keep away from combustible materials. Collect as oxidizer waste.
waste_disposal: Collect as oxidizer waste. Do not mix with organic waste.

first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air. Seek medical attention, especially if asthma symptoms develop.
first_aid.skin_contact: Wash with soap and water.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth. Seek medical attention.

regulatory_notes: Respiratory sensitizer. Workers with history of asthma should avoid handling. Used as free radical initiator for polyacrylamide gel polymerization.
